reasoning:
The question asks if the FRED series WORAL (Federal Reserve repurchase agreements, Wednesday level) will increase by Jan 25 and Feb 17 compared to its value on Jan 18, 2026. The latest available datum on Jan 18 is the Jan 14 observation, which is 1.0 (millions of USD). Because 1.0 is a very low baseline, any reading of 2.0 or higher constitutes an increase. 

Over the last 30 sampled weeks, 19 readings (63%) were >1.0, while 11 were ≤1.0 (either 0.0 or 1.0). In early January 2026, usage is at baseline levels (Jan 7 was 0.0, Jan 14 was 1.0). The short-term momentum suggests it is highly likely to remain at 0.0 or 1.0 for the Jan 21 observation (which resolves the Jan 25 date). I estimate a 42% chance that it bumps to >1.0 (such as a $2M test operation or higher usage).

For the Feb 17 resolution date, which will use the Feb 11 observation, there is more time for volatility and mid-quarter funding needs to return (e.g., in mid-Feb 2025, it spiked to 100.0). Accordingly, the probability of exceeding 1.0 reverts closer to the base rate. I estimate a 52% probability, which correctly reflects greater uncertainty (closer to 0.5) over the longer horizon.
